4608.name: Windows is starting up
4608.description: This event is logged when the Windows security subsystem starts.

4609.name: Windows is shutting down
4609.description: This event is logged when the Windows security subsystem shuts down.

4610.name: An authentication package has been loaded
4610.description: Logged when an authentication package is loaded by the Local Security Authority.

4611.name: A trusted logon process has been registered
4611.description: Logged when a trusted logon process registers with LSA.

4616.name: The system time was changed
4616.description: System time was modified. Could indicate tampering to evade time-based detections.

4624.name: An account was successfully logged on
4624.description: A logon was performed. Logon Type field indicates the type (2=interactive, 3=network, 4=batch, 5=service, 7=unlock, 8=NetworkCleartext, 9=NewCredentials, 10=RemoteInteractive, 11=CachedInteractive).

4625.name: An account failed to log on
4625.description: Failed logon attempt. Sub Status codes indicate the reason. Common: 0xC000006A (wrong password), 0xC0000064 (unknown user), 0xC000006D (bad credentials).

4627.name: Group membership information
4627.description: Supplement to 4624 — lists the groups the account is a member of at logon time.

4634.name: An account was logged off
4634.description: Account logoff. Logon ID links to the corresponding 4624 event.

4647.name: User initiated logoff
4647.description: User-initiated logoff. Generated when a user logs off interactively.

4648.name: A logon was attempted using explicit credentials
4648.description: Explicit credential usage (e.g. runas). May indicate lateral movement or privilege escalation.

4657.name: A registry value was modified
4657.description: A registry value was created or modified. Requires SACL auditing on registry keys.

4663.name: An attempt was made to access an object
4663.description: Object access attempt (file, folder, registry, etc.). Generates high volume — filter by Object Name.

4670.name: Permissions on an object were changed
4670.description: An object's DACL was changed. Can indicate tampering with file/folder permissions.

4672.name: Special privileges assigned to new logon
4672.description: A logon was granted sensitive privileges (SeDebugPrivilege, SeBackupPrivilege, etc.). Common with administrator accounts.

4673.name: A privileged service was called
4673.description: A privileged service call was made. Often logged during normal admin operations.

4674.name: An operation was attempted on a privileged object
4674.description: A privileged operation was attempted on an object.

4688.name: A new process has been created
4688.description: Process creation event. Includes process name, PID, parent PID, and command line (if policy enables it). Critical for detection.

4689.name: A process has exited
4689.description: Process exit event. Links to 4688 by Process ID.

4697.name: A service was installed in the system
4697.description: A new service was created. Often used for persistence.

4698.name: A scheduled task was created
4698.description: A new scheduled task was registered. Common persistence mechanism.

4700.name: A scheduled task was enabled
4700.description: A previously disabled scheduled task was re-enabled.

4702.name: A scheduled task was updated
4702.description: An existing scheduled task was modified. Review task action for suspicious commands.

4704.name: A user right was assigned
4704.description: A user right (privilege) was assigned to an account.

4705.name: A user right was removed
4705.description: A user right was removed from an account.

4719.name: System audit policy was changed
4719.description: Audit policy was modified. Could indicate an attacker disabling audit logging.

4720.name: A user account was created
4720.description: A new local user account was created. Review for unauthorized account creation.

4722.name: A user account was enabled
4722.description: A disabled user account was re-enabled.

4723.name: An attempt was made to change an account's password
4723.description: Password change attempt (by the user themselves).

4724.name: An attempt was made to reset an account's password

4726.name: A user account was deleted
4726.description: A user account was deleted. Check for suspicious deletion patterns.

4728.name: A member was added to a security-enabled global group
4728.description: User added to a global security group (e.g. Domain Admins). High-value for privilege escalation detection.

4729.name: A member was removed from a security-enabled global group
4729.description: User removed from a global security group.

4732.name: A member was added to a security-enabled local group
4732.description: User added to a local security group (e.g. Administrators).

4733.name: A member was removed from a security-enabled local group
4733.description: User removed from a local security group.

4738.name: A user account was changed
4738.description: User account attributes modified (e.g. description, SAM account name).

4740.name: A user account was locked out
4740.description: Account lockout due to too many failed logon attempts. Caller Computer Name indicates source of brute force.

4741.name: A computer account was created
4741.description: A new computer account was created in AD.

4743.name: A computer account was deleted
4743.description: A computer account was deleted from AD.

4756.name: A member was added to a security-enabled universal group
4756.description: User added to a universal security group.

4764.name: A group's type was changed
4764.description: A group type was changed (e.g. from distribution to security).

4765.name: SID History was added to an account
4765.description: SID history was added — can be used for privilege escalation in AD.

4766.name: An attempt to add SID History to an account failed
4766.description: Failed attempt to add SID history.

4768.name: A Kerberos authentication ticket (TGT) was requested
4768.description: Kerberos TGT request. Result code 0x12 indicates the account is disabled.

4769.name: A Kerberos service ticket was requested
4769.description: Kerberos TGS request. Ticket Encryption Type 0x17 (RC4) may indicate Kerberoasting.

4770.name: A Kerberos service ticket was renewed

4771.name: Kerberos pre-authentication failed
4771.description: Kerberos pre-auth failure. Failure Code 0x18 indicates wrong password. Bulk 4771s from one source indicate brute force.

4776.name: The domain controller attempted to validate the credentials for an account (NTLM)
4776.description: NTLM authentication attempt against a DC. Error code 0xC000006A = wrong password.

4778.name: A session was reconnected to a Window Station

4779.name: A session was disconnected from a Window Station

4781.name: The name of an account was changed
4781.description: Account was renamed. Can be used to evade detection based on account names.

4798.name: A user's local group membership was enumerated
4798.description: Local group membership was queried for a user. May indicate reconnaissance.

4799.name: A security-enabled local group membership was enumerated
4799.description: Local group membership was enumerated. May indicate reconnaissance.

4946.name: A rule was added to the Windows Firewall exception list
4946.description: A new firewall rule was added.

4947.name: A rule was modified in the Windows Firewall exception list
4947.description: An existing firewall rule was modified.

4948.name: A rule was deleted from the Windows Firewall exception list

4950.name: A Windows Firewall setting has changed

5001.name: Antimalware real-time protection configuration has changed
5001.description: Real-time protection was changed. May indicate defense evasion.

5140.name: A network share object was accessed
5140.description: Network share access. Filter by Share Name for sensitive shares.

5142.name: A network share object was added
5142.description: A new network share was created.

5145.name: A network share object was checked to see whether client can be granted desired access
5145.description: Detailed file share access. Very high volume — use only for targeted monitoring.

5156.name: The Windows Filtering Platform has permitted a connection
5156.description: Network connection was allowed by WFP. Includes source/dest IP and port.

5157.name: The Windows Filtering Platform has blocked a connection
5157.description: Network connection was blocked by WFP.

5379.name: Credential Manager credentials were read
5379.description: Credential Manager was accessed. May indicate credential dumping.

7034.name: Service crashed unexpectedly
7034.description: A service terminated unexpectedly. May indicate process injection or crashes from AV.

7036.name: Service entered stopped/running state
7036.description: Service state change. Monitor for security-relevant services being stopped.

7040.name: Service start type was changed
7040.description: Service startup type was modified (e.g. disabled to auto-start). Persistence indicator.

7045.name: A new service was installed in the system
7045.description: New service installed (System event log). Complements 4697 from Security log.

1.name: Process Create
1.description: Process creation with full command line, hash, and parent process. The most useful Sysmon event for detection.

2.name: File Creation Time Changed
2.description: A process changed a file creation time. Timestomping indicator.

3.name: Network Connection
3.description: Network connection initiated by a process. Includes source/dest IP, port, and process name.

5.name: Process Terminated
5.description: Process exit. Links to Event ID 1 by ProcessGuid.

6.name: Driver Loaded
6.description: A driver was loaded into the kernel. Review for unsigned or malicious drivers.

7.name: Image Loaded
7.description: A DLL was loaded. High volume — typically filtered to specific DLLs or processes.

8.name: CreateRemoteThread
8.description: A thread was created in a remote process. Strong process injection indicator.

9.name: RawAccessRead
9.description: A process used raw disk access. May indicate data theft or disk imaging.

10.name: ProcessAccess
10.description: A process opened another process. LSASS access (GrantedAccess 0x1010) indicates credential dumping.

11.name: FileCreate
11.description: A file was created or overwritten. Monitor for files dropped in temp/startup locations.

12.name: RegistryEvent (Create/Delete)
12.description: Registry key or value was created or deleted.

13.name: RegistryEvent (Set Value)
13.description: Registry value was set. Monitor Run keys, services, and autostart locations.

14.name: RegistryEvent (Rename Key/Value)
14.description: Registry key or value was renamed.

15.name: FileCreateStreamHash
15.description: A named file stream (ADS) was created. Common for hiding payloads.

16.name: Sysmon Config State Changed
16.description: Sysmon configuration was changed. May indicate an attacker modifying Sysmon to reduce visibility.

17.name: PipeEvent (Pipe Created)
17.description: A named pipe was created. Some malware and C2 frameworks use named pipes for IPC.

18.name: PipeEvent (Pipe Connected)
18.description: A connection to a named pipe was made.

19.name: WmiEvent (WmiEventFilter activity detected)
19.description: WMI event filter was registered. WMI subscriptions are a persistence mechanism.

21.name: WmiEvent (WmiEventConsumerToFilter activity detected)
21.description: WMI consumer was bound to a filter.

22.name: DNSEvent (DNS query)
22.description: A DNS query was made by a process. Useful for detecting C2 beaconing by domain.

23.name: FileDelete (File Deletion Archived)
23.description: A file was deleted and archived in the Sysmon archive folder.

24.name: ClipboardChange
24.description: Clipboard content changed. Requires Sysmon v12+.

25.name: ProcessTampering
25.description: Process image was modified (hollowing, herky). Strong malware indicator.

26.name: FileDeleteDetected (File Deletion Logged)
26.description: A file was deleted (logged but not archived).

27.name: FileBlockExecutable
27.description: Sysmon blocked an executable from being written based on config rules.

29.name: FileExecutableDetected
29.description: A new executable file was detected in a monitored location.

15003.name: MSExchange Front End HTTP Proxy
15003.description: Front end proxy request. Includes authenticated user and source IP. Check for OWA anomalies.

18456.name: Login failed for user
18456.description: SQL Server failed login. State code indicates reason: 1=generic, 5=invalid user, 8=wrong password, 16=not allowed on this SQL.

18452.name: Login failed — untrusted domain
18452.description: Login attempt from an untrusted domain.

15457.name: Configuration option changed
15457.description: SQL Server configuration was changed (e.g. xp_cmdshell enabled).
